questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The bureaucracy in a democratic country is a separate centre of power.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has the power to make decisions independently without being accountable to the elected representatives.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Explanation: This is because the bureaucracy has significant influence in policy-making and implementation, and is not directly accountable to the people.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The judiciary is a separate centre of power in a democratic country.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has the power to interpret the Constitution and laws of the land.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Explanation: This is because the judiciary plays a crucial role in protecting the rights of citizens and ensuring that the government does not overstep its powers.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The military is a separate centre of power in a democratic country.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has the power to use force to maintain national security.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Explanation: This is because the military has significant influence in national security and foreign policy, and is accountable only to the government.
